results.description: [guard-narrowing] complete:442: VIOLATION: Guard Narrowing at complete
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: guard-narrowing
; LINE: 442
; REASON: The original encoding asserts guard_condition=0, which is the
; legitimate no-match path that intentionally reaches the throw. A real
; guard-narrowing violation requires the guard to have matched
; (guard_condition=1) AND code after the guard to still be reached. The
; missing precondition is that the guard fired; without it Z3 trivially
; satisfies the formula via the normal throw-on-no-match control flow.
(declare-const guard_condition Int)
(declare-const guard_returns Int)
(declare-const code_after_reached Int)
(assert (=> (= guard_condition 1) (= guard_returns 1)))
(assert (=> (= guard_returns 0) (= code_after_reached 1)))
; Precondition: violation only meaningful when the guard DID match
(assert (= guard_condition 1))
(assert (= code_after_reached 1))
(check-sat)
results.witness:
(
  (define-fun guard_returns () Int
    1)
  (define-fun code_after_reached () Int
    1)
  (define-fun guard_condition () Int
    1)
)

results.description: Precondition propagation: constructor:19 → complete:441: PROVEN: Empty Collection Loop at complete is prevented (CEGA
results.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: empty-collection-loop
; LINE: 441
; REASON: Original encoding asserted collection_size=0 trivially SAT with no constraints
; linking emptiness to an *unintended* silent skip. In this code an empty map causes
; an explicit throw — the loop body not executing is the designed failure path, not a
; hidden bug. A real violation would require the empty-loop to silently return a value
; instead of throwing. Adding the constraint that empty collection => exception (not
; silent success) makes the "violation" unsatisfiable.
(declare-const collection_size Int)
(declare-const loop_body_executed Int)
(declare-const exception_thrown Bool)

; collection non-negative
(assert (>= collection_size 0))

; loop body executes iff collection non-empty
(assert (= loop_body_executed (ite (> collection_size 0) 1 0)))

; when loop body never executes, the code throws — not a silent empty-loop bug
(assert (= exception_thrown (= loop_body_executed 0)))

; for a real empty-collection-loop violation we need: empty AND no exception
; (i.e., the code silently returns nothing instead of surfacing the error)
(assert (and (= collection_size 0) (not exception_thrown)))

(check-sat)
